title: Hebbian counters increment regardless of updateEdge success

findingDetails.evidence: Lines 362-366: `updateEdge(...)` then `strengthened++` with no check of its boolean return; decay path is identical at 379-381 (`updateEdge(...)` then `decayed++`). updateEdge (causal-edges.ts:824) returns false on changes===0, non-finite strength, or a caught SQLITE error.
findingDetails.why: On any updateEdge failure the reported ConsolidationResult.hebbian {strengthened,decayed} overstates the number of edges actually mutated — the returned telemetry diverges from DB state.
findingDetails.recommendation: Gate the increment on the return: `if (updateEdge(edge.id, {strength: cappedStrength}, 'hebbian', 'hebbian-strengthening')) strengthened++;` and likewise for decayed.

title: Swallowed Hebbian failure still commits last_run_at as a completed cycle

findingDetails.evidence: runHebbianCycle wraps writes in `database.transaction(()=>{...})()` (344) but its outer try/catch (384-387) swallows the re-thrown savepoint error and returns counts. The caller runConsolidationCycleIfEnabled then unconditionally runs `INSERT ... consolidation_state ... last_run_at = datetime('now')` (525-529) and COMMITs (531-534). better-sqlite3 nests the inner transaction as a SAVEPOINT, so a throw rolls back only the hebbian writes while the outer BEGIN IMMEDIATE survives.
findingDetails.why: A failed strengthen/decay pass (rolled back to savepoint) is invisible to the caller and is recorded as a successful run, suppressing the next consolidation for CONSOLIDATION_INTERVAL_DAYS (7) days. Partial failure is silently booked as success.
findingDetails.recommendation: Have runHebbianCycle signal failure (return a status or re-throw) so runConsolidationCycleIfEnabled can skip the last_run_at advance (or roll back the whole outer transaction) when the hebbian phase did not complete.

title: Read-only O(n^2) contradiction scan holds the BEGIN IMMEDIATE write lock

findingDetails.evidence: runConsolidationCycleIfEnabled does `database.exec('BEGIN IMMEDIATE')` (489) then runConsolidationCycle (523), whose first step scanContradictions (441) runs a pairwise loop over up to 500 memories with a 5s deadline (149-160), followed by the N+1 per-node bounds count (453-464). All of this read-only work executes while the RESERVED write lock is held.
findingDetails.why: Other writers — including the epic's new multi-process CLI front-doors (.opencode/bin/*.cjs) sharing the DB — are blocked for the entire scan (up to 5s+), risking SQLITE_BUSY on concurrent memory writes during a cycle. The scan needs no write lock.
findingDetails.recommendation: Run the read-only scan/cluster/bounds work before BEGIN IMMEDIATE, then take the immediate lock only for the cadence re-check + runHebbianCycle + last_run_at write; or narrow the lock to just the mutating phase while keeping the cadence guard.

title: Threaded database param diverges from module-global handle used for writes

findingDetails.evidence: detectStaleEdges(_database) ignores its parameter (underscore) and calls getStaleEdges (causal-edges.ts:1136) which uses the module-level `db`. Same split inside runHebbianCycle: SELECTs use the threaded `database` (346,370) but updateEdge (363,379) and checkEdgeBounds->countEdgesForNode (462,418) operate on causal-edges' module `db` set by init(). Caller passes requireDb() (response-builder.ts:852).
findingDetails.why: The atomicity of runHebbianCycle's `database.transaction()` (on the param) only covers its writes because the module-global happens to be the same connection. If init() ever set a different handle, the SELECTs and writes would run on different connections — the transaction would not cover the writes, and the BEGIN IMMEDIATE on the param connection would self-conflict (SQLITE_BUSY/deadlock) against writes on the global.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make consolidation use a single handle consistently — either drop the unused `database` params and rely solely on the module-global, or thread the handle into updateEdge/countEdgesForNode/getStaleEdges so reads and writes provably share one connection.
